data.title: Vitalhub announces recommended cash acquisition of Induction Healthcare Group
data.description:
• VitalHub (OTCQX:VHIBF) has agreed the terms and conditions of a recommended cash acquisition to acquire all the issued and to be issued share capital of Induction Healthcare Group.
  • Under the terms of the Acquisition, each Induction shareholder will be entitled to receive £0.10 in cash for each Induction share held, valuing Induction at ~£9.7M.

data.title: Terreno Realty acquires Redmond, Washington-based property for ~$9.3M
data.description:
• Industrial REIT Terreno Realty (NYSE:TRNO) announced on Thursday the acquisition of a Redmond, Washington-based property for ~$9.3M.
  • The property consists of one industrial distribution building of ~33,000 sq. ft. on 1.5 acres. It is set to be renovated to ~26,000 sq. ft. after the existing tenant vacates.

data.title: SmartRent CEO Shane Paladin steps down
data.description:
• SmartRent (NYSE:SMRT) said on Thursday that at the request of the board, Shane Paladin has stepped down from his positions as President and CEO, and resigned from the board, effective immediately.
  • John Dorman, Chairman of the board, has been appointed interim CEO and Alison Dean has been appointed lead independent director. 
  • The board has reinitiated its search with an executive search firm to assist in finding SmartRent’s next CEO and is already in the process of identifying and evaluating candidates. Paladin will remain with the company in an advisory role for three months to ensure a smooth transition.
  • The company also provided preliminary first quarter 2025 results. The company expects first quarter 2025 total revenue of $40.5-$41.5 million, a decrease of 18-20% from the same period last year.

data.title: Dexcom's continuous glucose monitoring system gets FDA clearance
data.description:
• DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM) said on Thursday that the FDA has cleared the Dexcom G7 15 Day continuous glucose monitoring system for people over the age of 18 with diabetes.
  • Stock is up about 8.8% premarket.
  • Dexcom G7 15 Day is expected to launch in the U.S. in the second half of 2025, the company said.

data.title: Amazon sellers will pass tariff costs on to customers: CEO
data.description:
Andy Jassy, CEO of retail giant Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), has said its third-party sellers may “past costs on” related to US President Donald Trump’s tariffs to customers.

Jassy made the remarks in an interview on CNBC on April 10, noting that the company was still evaluating the impact of Trump’s tariffs.

“I understand why, I mean, depending on which country you’re in, you don’t have 50% extra margin that you can play with,” Jassy said in the interview, explaining why sellers would pass on the costs.

Trump most recently issued a 90-day pause on his “reciprocal” tariffs but retained the 10% base rate and ratcheted up levies against China to 125%. Tariffs on steel and aluminum imports also remain at 25%.

The head of Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) also said the company has made strategic inventory purchases and is moving to renegotiate certain purchase orders to mitigate the impact of tariffs.

data.title: What if Apple doesn't raise like-for-like iPhone prices? Morgan Stanley answers
data.description:
Morgan Stanley expects Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to significantly minimize the tariff headwind and mitigate 125% China levies, with fast-ramping India production and targeted iPhone mix shift.

Since tariffs on Chinese imports were first introduced in early March, the expectation has been that Apple will be forced to raise iPhone prices to offset the consistently increasing cost of tariffs on Chinese imports.

Recent production data and news reports also support that Apple has pulled forward inventory and redirected some production from India to minimize the impact of tariffs on U.S.-bound products.

However, Morgan Stanley doesn't believe Apple is solely betting on a presidential exemption (potential company-specific tariff carve-outs) and the following steps could be a less disruptive path that Apple could take: 

  • With the 90-day reciprocal tariff reprieve, Apple will look to its ODM partners in India – Hon Hai, Pegatron, and Tata – and communicate the desire to significantly ramp production in India to help meet US iPhone demand.
  • Gross margin on the highest-end iPhone storage SKU is 10-15 points higher than the lowest storage SKU, and therefore, if Apple can shift demand towards higher-margin iPhone models, it can lessen the blow from China tariffs as India production also ramps.
  • At the same time, Apple could leverage financing plans, and higher carrier subsidies/trade-in values to help offset the synthetically higher iPhone prices.
  • What if Apple extended the Apple Card iPhone installment plan to 36 months? For a $1,099 iPhone, that would cut the monthly iPhone payment 33%, from $45 to $30, and also drive more users to the Apple Card.

To conclude, with the largest base of pent-up iPhone demand ever, new AI features rolling out (slowly) around the world, and investments in cloud, and payments, the Bank believes Apple can accelerate iPhone growth starting in FY27.

data.title: Newsmax falsely reported on Dominion's involvement in 2020 election rigging - Delaware Judge
data.description:
A judge in Delaware ruled that conservative network Newsmax Media (NYSE:NMAX) defamed and made false statements against Dominion Voting Systems by alleging the electronic voting hardware and software firm was involved in rigging the 2020 presidential election, which Donald Trump lost to Joe Biden.

Delaware Superior Court Judge Eric Davis is still on the fence whether the news outlet has to pay damages for the defamation in the $1.6B suit filed by Dominion.

"Newsmax covered both sides of the 2020 election dispute fairly. At no time did it defame Dominion. This case represents a serious threat to free speech and a free press, and Newsmax will defend itself vigorously at trial,” a spokesperson for the pro-Trump news company said Wednesday.

A representative for the voting company said, "We are gratified by the court’s thorough ruling."

The possibility of a settlement outside court is still on the table. In 2023, Dominion won a similar lawsuit against Fox (NASDAQ:FOXA) and got $787M in damages.

Shares of Newsmax have seen volatile surges and declines since going public on March 28. NMAX closed down 11.5% on Wednesday and is down 2% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: UK antitrust regulator may accept SLB-ChampionX remedies
data.description:
The UK's Competition and Markets Authority said it may accept the remedies offered by SLB (NYSE:SLB) and oilfield services company ChampionX  (NASDAQ:CHX) for their planned deal. 

The CMA said that there are "reasonable grounds" to believe that the undertakings offered by SLB and ChampionX (NASDAQ:CHX) may be accepted by the CMA under the Enterprise ACT 2002, according to a statement the regulator made on Thursday.

SLB has offered to sell its chemical technologies business in the UK and ChampionX (CHX) said in February that it will sell its equity interests in US Synthetic. 

The CMA has until June 11 to make a final decision on the deal, though it can be extended until Aug. 8. 

SLB, formerly Schlumberger, announced last April that it agreed to acquire ChampionX (CHX) in an all-stock deal, saying it will strengthen its leadership position in oilfield services with world-class production chemicals and artificial lift technologies.

data.title: Neptune Digital Assets expands Bitcoin treasury to over 400 BTC
data.description:
• Neptune Digital Assets (OTCQB:NPPTF) on Thursday announced that it has expanded its Bitcoin treasury to 401 BTC.
  • Neptune's average acquisition price per Bitcoin is $31,564.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tetra Tech awarded £36M contract for water infrastructure services
data.description:
• Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK) said on Thursday that Severn Trent Water has selected Tetra Tech for a £36M multiple-award contract to provide comprehensive water infrastructure services in the United Kingdom.
  • Under the 3-year contract, Tetra Tech engineers will conduct geotechnical investigations to assess soil and groundwater conditions to support the development of Severn Trent Water’s capital improvements projects. 
  • Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK) shares were down 1.57% in pre-market trading.
